reason: The assistant uses tools correctly, stays grounded in tool outputs, and handles failures honestly (e.g., when wallet addresses are missing). The user asks natural follow-up questions about account status, whale wallets, and paper trading, though the phrasing 'I'd also like to know:' is slightly formal. The planner set up a task requiring whale wallet addresses that don't exist in the system, creating an inherent limitation, but the assistant navigates this well by explaining constraints and providing comprehensive educational content. No hallucinations or contradictions with tool data. The task is mostly completed - the user received all requested information even though actual trading couldn't execute without wallet addresses.
